title: 2 Tips to Save Context Space in Claude Code
description: Learn to audit your Claude Code context usage and reduce token consumption by splitting docs and fixing MCP tools.

cards.title: Optimize Claude Code Context Usage
cards.body: You'll learn two proven techniques to dramatically reduce token consumption in Claude Code by auditing context usage and fixing common bloat sources.

cards.title: The Context Audit Approach
cards.body: The key to optimization is visibility. You need to see exactly what's consuming your tokens before you can fix it.

analogy.text: Like checking your phone's battery usage to find apps draining power, you need a breakdown of what's eating your Claude context.

cards.title: Generate a Context Usage Table
cards.body: Add this simple request to the end of any prompt to see exactly where your tokens are going.
cards.code: When done, list the processes and tools and create a table showing what used context space.
cards.codeCaption: Claude Code will analyze and show you a breakdown of token usage

source.author: AI Coding Daily
source.description: Seeing the context usage breakdown table in action

cards.title: The Big Document Problem
cards.body: When you reference a large documentation file in your prompt, Claude loads the entire document into context, even if you only need one section.
cards.warn: A 1,000-line project doc can consume 15% of your context space, even when you only want to work on one phase

cards.title: Split Large Docs Into Separate Files
cards.body: Instead of one massive documentation file, create individual markdown files for each section or phase.
bullets: Keep each file focused on one specific area
Aim for 160-200 lines per file maximum

cards.title: Quick Check
cards.question: What happens when you prompt Claude to 'work on phase 1 from project-phases.md' if that file contains all project phases?

options.text: Claude only loads phase 1 into context
Claude loads the entire document into context first
Claude asks you to specify which section to load
Claude automatically splits the document for you

cards.correctFeedback: Exactly! Claude loads everything first, then filters what it needs.
cards.wrongFeedback: Not quite. Claude actually loads the entire document into context, regardless of which section you want to work on.

cards.title: Reference Specific Files in Prompts
cards.body: Update your prompting approach to reference only the specific file you need.
cards.codeCaption: This loads ~160 lines instead of 1,000+ lines into context

source.author: AI Coding Daily
source.description: Comparing context usage before and after splitting documents

cards.title: Great Progress!
cards.body: You've learned how to audit context usage and optimize large documents. Now let's tackle the second major context consumer: MCP tools.

cards.title: MCP Tools Can Over-Fetch
cards.body: MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools sometimes pull more data than needed. A database schema tool might fetch entire databases when you only need the current one.
cards.warn: An over-fetching MCP tool consumed 12,000 tokens (significant percentage) in the example project

cards.title: Override MCP Behavior in Claude.md
cards.body: You can add specific instructions to your Claude.md file to control how MCP tools behave.
cards.code: When using database schema, filter only current database.
cards.codeCaption: This instruction tells Claude to limit the MCP tool's data fetching
bullets: Add override instructions for problematic MCP tools
Be specific about what data to include/exclude

cards.title: Verify the Fix
cards.body: Run your context audit again to confirm the MCP optimization worked.

source.author: AI Coding Daily
source.description: Seeing the dramatic reduction in MCP tool context usage after filtering

cards.safe: After the fix, database schema usage dropped to just 0.5% of context and saved 3% total

cards.title: Knowledge Check
cards.question: Where should you add override instructions for MCP tools that are over-fetching data?
cards.correctFeedback: Perfect! Claude.md is where you set persistent instructions for how Claude should behave.
cards.wrongFeedback: Not quite. The Claude.md file is where you add persistent instructions that apply to all your prompts.

cards.title: Run Regular Context Audits
cards.body: Make context auditing a regular practice, not a one-time fix.
bullets: Run the audit across 5-10 different prompts
Look for recurring patterns, not just one-off issues
Check context usage when adding new MCP tools
Monitor after making changes to your workflow

cards.title: The Results Speak
cards.body: These two simple changes can have dramatic impact on your context efficiency.
bullets: Document splitting: Reduced from 15% to 1% context usage
MCP filtering: Dropped from 12,000+ tokens to 0.5% usage
Total savings: Over 3% of total context space recovered

cards.title: Context Optimization Master!
cards.body: You now know how to audit Claude Code context usage and apply two proven techniques to dramatically reduce token consumption. Your future projects will be much more efficient!
